prophecies.a:
天有眼，地有眼，人人都有一双眼。天也翻，地也翻，逍遥自在乐无边。

白话文翻译：天有天的法眼，地有地的法眼，每个人也都有一双眼睛。天翻地覆之后，便是逍遥自在、快乐无边的新世界。
prophecies.q_en: Opening: Heaven and Earth Have Eyes
prophecies.a_en:
Heaven has eyes, earth has eyes, every person has a pair of eyes. Heaven overturns, earth overturns — then comes carefree joy without end.

Vernacular: Heaven has its all-seeing eye, earth has its all-seeing eye, and every person also has a pair of eyes. After the world is turned upside down, a new era of carefree and boundless joy awaits.

prophecies.a:
贫者一万留一千，富者一万留二三。贫富若不回心转，看看死期在眼前。

白话文翻译：穷人一万人中能留下一千人，富人一万人中只能留下两三人。无论贫富，如果不回心转意，死亡就在眼前。
prophecies.a_en:
Of ten thousand poor, a thousand survive; of ten thousand rich, only two or three remain. If rich and poor do not change their hearts, death stares them in the face.

Vernacular: Among ten thousand poor people, a thousand may survive; among ten thousand rich, only two or three will remain. Regardless of wealth, if one does not repent and reform, death is imminent.

prophecies.a:
平地无有五谷种，谨防四野绝人烟。若问瘟疫何时现，但看九冬十月间。

白话文翻译：平地上种不出五谷粮食，要提防四方田野荒无人烟。如果问瘟疫什么时候出现，就看那农历九月、十月的冬天。
prophecies.a_en:
On flat land no grain can be sown; beware — the four wilds will be devoid of people. If you ask when the plague appears, look to the ninth and tenth winter months.

Vernacular: No grain will grow on the flatlands; beware that the countryside will become utterly deserted. If you ask when the plague will arrive, look to the winter months of the ninth and tenth lunar months.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「九冬十月间」与2019年末新冠疫情首次出现的时间（约2019年11-12月，对应农历九/十月）存在时间上的巧合。但碑记所述的「平地无有五谷种」「四野绝人烟」等极端景象并未在现实中完全发生，瘟疫的严重程度也远未达到碑文描述的末世级别。
prophecies.verdict_en: The timing of 'ninth and tenth winter months' coincidentally aligns with the initial emergence of COVID-19 around November–December 2019 (corresponding to the 9th/10th lunar months). However, the extreme imagery of 'no grain on flatlands' and 'deserted countryside' did not fully materialize, and the pandemic's severity, while significant, did not reach the apocalyptic scale described in the inscription.

prophecies.a:
行善之人可保全，作恶之人不久长。十愁难过猪鼠年，一愁天下乱纷纷，二愁东西饿死人，三愁湖广遭大难，四愁各省起狼烟，五愁人民不安然，六愁九冬十月间，七愁有饭无人食，八愁有人无衣穿，九愁尸体无人捡，十愁难过猪鼠年。

白话文翻译：行善之人可以保全性命，作恶之人活不长久。十种忧愁难以度过猪年和鼠年：一愁天下大乱，二愁东西方饿死人，三愁湖广地区遭受大难，四愁各省烽烟四起，五愁百姓不得安宁，六愁九月十月冬天，七愁有饭却无人吃，八愁有人却无衣穿，九愁尸体无人收殓，十愁难以度过猪年和鼠年。
prophecies.q_en: Ten Sorrows: Year of the Pig and Rat
prophecies.a_en:
Those who do good shall be preserved; evildoers will not last long. Ten sorrows hard to survive in the year of Pig and Rat. First sorrow: chaos across the world. Second sorrow: people starving east and west. Third sorrow: great calamity in Hu-Guang. Fourth sorrow: smoke of war in every province. Fifth sorrow: people cannot find peace. Sixth sorrow: the ninth and tenth winter months. Seventh sorrow: food but none to eat it. Eighth sorrow: people but none with clothing. Ninth sorrow: corpses with none to collect them. Tenth sorrow: hard to survive the year of Pig and Rat.

Vernacular: Those who do good will survive; evildoers will not last long. Ten sorrows are hard to endure through the Years of the Pig and Rat: the first is worldwide chaos; the second, starvation east and west; the third, great disaster in the Hu-Guang region; the fourth, war smoke rising in every province; the fifth, people living in fear; the sixth, the winter months of the ninth and tenth months; the seventh, food with no one to eat it; the eighth, people with no clothes to wear; the ninth, corpses with no one to bury them; the tenth, the impossibility of surviving the Pig and Rat years.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「猪鼠年」对应2019年（猪年）和2020年（鼠年），恰好是新冠疫情爆发和全球蔓延的时间。「三愁湖广遭大难」中「湖广」在古代指湖北湖南一带，COVID-19首先在湖北武汉爆发，地理吻合度较高。但碑记中描述的其他灾象（饿死人、各省狼烟、尸体无人捡等）在现实中并未达到所述程度。部分时间和地理信息存在巧合，但整体灾难规模与描述不符。
prophecies.verdict_en: The 'Year of Pig and Rat' corresponds to 2019 (Pig) and 2020 (Rat), precisely when COVID-19 emerged and spread globally. 'Great calamity in Hu-Guang' — Hu-Guang historically refers to the Hubei-Hunan region, and COVID-19 first broke out in Wuhan, Hubei, showing notable geographic alignment. However, other calamities described (mass starvation, war in every province, uncollected corpses) did not materialize to the described extent. Some temporal and geographic details align coincidentally, but the overall scale of disaster does not match the description.

prophecies.a:
若得过了大劫年，才算世间不老仙。就是铜打铁罗汉，难过七月初一十三。任你金刚铁罗汉，除非善乃能保全。

白话文翻译：如果能度过这大劫之年，才算得上是世间的不老神仙。即便你是铜铸铁打的罗汉，也难过七月初一到十三。任凭你是金刚铁罗汉，除非行善才能保全性命。
prophecies.a_en:
If you can survive the great tribulation year, you are truly an immortal of the world. Even an iron-cast Arhat cannot survive the first to the thirteenth of the seventh month. No matter if you are a diamond Arhat of iron, only through virtue can you be preserved.

Vernacular: If you can survive the great tribulation year, you can be considered an ageless immortal. Even if you were an Arhat cast from bronze and iron, you cannot survive the first to the thirteenth of the seventh month. No matter how indestructible you are, only through doing good can your life be preserved.

prophecies.a:
谨防人人艰难过，关过天番龙蛇年。幼儿好似朱洪武，四川更比汉中苦。

白话文翻译：要谨防人人都难以度过的艰难时期，关键要度过天翻地覆的龙年和蛇年。到那时幼小的孩童好比朱元璋幼时的苦难，四川的困苦更甚于汉中。
prophecies.q_en: Heed the Warning: Years of the Dragon and Snake
prophecies.a_en:
Beware — everyone will struggle to get through; the critical passage is the heaven-overturning years of Dragon and Snake. Young children will suffer like the young Zhu Hongwu; Sichuan will be more bitter than Hanzhong.

Vernacular: Beware that everyone will face great hardship; the crucial test is the world-overturning years of the Dragon and Snake. At that time, young children will suffer like the young Zhu Yuanzhang; the hardship in Sichuan will surpass that of Hanzhong.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「龙蛇年」对应2024年（龙年）和2025年（蛇年）。截至2026年初，2024-2025年间并未出现碑文所述的「人人艰难过」「天翻地覆」等重大灾难，四川也未发生超出正常范围的特别苦难。此段预言与现实不符。
prophecies.verdict_en: The 'Dragon and Snake years' correspond to 2024 (Dragon) and 2025 (Snake). As of early 2026, no catastrophic events matching the described 'everyone struggling' or 'heaven overturning' occurred during 2024–2025, nor did Sichuan experience exceptional suffering beyond normal levels. This prophecy does not match reality.

prophecies.a:
大狮吼如雷，胜过悼妈啼。犀牛现出尾，平地遇猛若。若问大平年，架桥迎新主。上元甲子到，人人哈哈笑。

白话文翻译：巨大的狮子吼声如雷鸣，胜过丧母的哭泣。犀牛露出了尾巴，平地上遇到了猛兽。如果要问太平盛世什么时候来到，那就是架起桥梁迎接新的主人。等到上元甲子来到的时候，人人都会开心大笑。
prophecies.q_en: Apocalyptic Visions: Darkness Over the Land
prophecies.a_en:
The great lion roars like thunder, louder than a mourning mother's cry. The rhinoceros reveals its tail; on flat ground one meets a fierce beast. If you ask when the great peace comes, a bridge is built to welcome the new lord. When the first Jiazi cycle arrives, everyone laughs with joy.

Vernacular: The great lion roars like thunder, surpassing even the wailing of a bereaved mother. The rhinoceros reveals its tail; ferocious beasts appear on flat ground. If you ask when the era of great peace will arrive, it is when a bridge is built to welcome the new lord. When the first Jiazi cycle comes, everyone will laugh with joy.

prophecies.a:
问他猪鼠年，大灾在眼前。人人善作急，亦叫人人哭。白骨流成河，伤心日月食。

白话文翻译：问起那猪年和鼠年，大灾难就在眼前。人人急着行善，却又免不了人人痛哭。白骨堆积成河，令人伤心的日食月食频频出现。
prophecies.q_en: Moral Warning: Cultivate Virtue to Survive
prophecies.a_en:
Ask about the year of Pig and Rat — great disaster is before your eyes. Everyone rushes to do good, yet everyone weeps. White bones flow like a river; the heart grieves as sun and moon are eclipsed.

Vernacular: Ask about the Years of the Pig and Rat — great calamity is imminent. Everyone hastens to do good, yet everyone also weeps. White bones pile up like a river; hearts break as eclipses of sun and moon appear.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 再次提及「猪鼠年」，与新冠疫情时间吻合。全球确有大规模死亡，世界各地民众确实经历了悲痛。但「白骨流成河」的极端描述远超实际情况。碑文的末世意象与疫情的严重性有程度上的差距。
prophecies.verdict_en: Again references the 'Year of Pig and Rat,' coinciding with the COVID-19 timeline. There was indeed large-scale death globally and widespread grief. However, the extreme imagery of 'white bones flowing like a river' far exceeds what actually occurred. The apocalyptic imagery in the inscription far surpasses the actual severity of the pandemic.

prophecies.a:
早晨过了是中秋，过了中秋白骨堆。有人行善暗中乐，有人行恶把命催。有人转世人变畜，有人行善升天梯。

白话文翻译：早晨过去就到了中秋，中秋过后白骨堆积如山。有人行善暗中快乐，有人作恶催命而亡。有人转世投胎变成畜生，有人行善修行升上天梯。
prophecies.a_en:
Morning passes and it is Mid-Autumn; past Mid-Autumn, white bones pile up. Some do good and rejoice in secret; some do evil and hasten their death. Some are reborn as beasts in the next life; some do good and ascend the heavenly ladder.

Vernacular: Morning passes and it is already Mid-Autumn; after Mid-Autumn, white bones pile up like mountains. Some who do good find quiet joy; some who do evil rush toward death. Some are reborn as animals in the next life; some who do good ascend the heavenly ladder.

prophecies.a:
大水冲得路不平，火烧长安日夜明。一切食粮都涨价，走路才是上人行。

白话文翻译：大水冲毁了道路使之坑洼不平，火烧长安城日夜通明。一切粮食都涨价昂贵，只有步行才是上等人的出行方式。
prophecies.q_en: Natural and Man-Made Disasters: Flood and Fire
prophecies.a_en:
Great floods wash the roads uneven; fire burns Chang'an bright day and night. All food prices soar; walking on foot is the way of the worthy.

Vernacular: Great floods wash away the roads, leaving them uneven; fire burns the city of Chang'an bright through day and night. All grain prices soar; only those who walk on foot are considered respectable.

prophecies.a:
世上有人行大善，遭了此劫不上算。众生都要仔细看，大灾过后有新天。人人都是换一番，世界处处光明现。

白话文翻译：世上有人行大善之事，遭遇了这场劫难却不算什么。众人都要仔细看清楚，大灾过后会有新的天地。人人都焕然一新，世界处处呈现光明。
prophecies.q_en: After the Tribulation: A New Era of Peace
prophecies.a_en:
In the world some do great good — enduring this tribulation is nothing to them. All beings should look carefully: after the great disaster comes a new heaven. Everyone is renewed; the world shines with light everywhere.

Vernacular: Some in the world perform great acts of good — for them, enduring this tribulation is of no consequence. All living beings should observe carefully: after the great disaster, a new heaven and earth will emerge. Everyone will be renewed, and the world will shine with light in every direction.

prophecies.a:
人人喜笑人人乐，凡事不用再奔波。能逢木兔方为寿，泽及群生乐如何。有人识得其中意，富贵荣华百世昌。

白话文翻译：人人欢喜大笑人人快乐，凡事不用再四处奔波。能够等到兔年就算长寿，恩泽遍及众生何等快乐。有人能领悟其中的深意，便可富贵荣华百世昌盛。
prophecies.q_en: Conclusion: Exhortation to Return to Virtue
prophecies.a_en:
Everyone laughs in joy, everyone is happy; no more need to toil and wander. To live until the year of the Wood Rabbit is true longevity; blessings spread to all beings — what joy! Those who grasp the meaning within shall enjoy prosperity for a hundred generations.

Vernacular: Everyone laughs and rejoices; there is no more need to toil and run about. To survive until the Year of the Wood Rabbit is true longevity; blessings extend to all living beings — what happiness! Those who understand the deeper meaning shall enjoy wealth, honor, and glory for a hundred generations.

prophecies.a:
繁华市镇变荒丘，万户萧疏鬼唱愁。活着不如死去好，挑宽担重压肩头。

白话文翻译：繁华的城镇变成了荒丘废墟，万户人家萧条冷落只有鬼魂在唱着忧愁。活着还不如死去好，挑着宽大沉重的担子压在肩头。
prophecies.q_en: Post-Plague World: Soaring Prices and Ruin
prophecies.a_en:
Bustling towns become desolate mounds; ten thousand households lie empty, ghosts sing of sorrow. Living is no better than dying; heavy burdens press upon the shoulders.

Vernacular: Prosperous towns turn into barren mounds; ten thousand households stand desolate as ghostly wails of sorrow fill the air. Being alive is no better than being dead; heavy burdens press down upon one's shoulders.

prophecies.a:
天下同心一道行，万教归一正乾坤。人有一分善念在，灾殃面前有神帮。人有一分恶念存，难逃大难必来临。

白话文翻译：天下人同心走一条正道，万教归一以正乾坤。人若有一分善念在心中，灾难面前就有神灵相助。人若存一分恶念，就难逃大难必然降临。
prophecies.q_en: The Righteous Lord Descends: Order Restored
prophecies.a_en:
The whole world walks one path with one heart; ten thousand teachings return to one, setting heaven and earth right. If a person holds even one thought of goodness, before disaster the gods will help. If a person harbors even one thought of evil, great calamity will surely come.

Vernacular: The whole world walks the same righteous path with united hearts; all religions return to one, restoring order to heaven and earth. If a person holds even a single thought of goodness, the gods will help them in the face of disaster. If a person harbors even a single evil thought, great calamity will inevitably befall them.
